signals.headline: US and Iran Weighing Extended Ceasefire Deal Before April 21 Expiration | PENDING | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
US and Iran Weighing Extended Ceasefire Deal Before April 21 Expiration | PENDING | HIGH confidence**

The US and Iran are actively negotiating to extend their two-week ceasefire beyond its April 21 expiration date.[1] Trump is simultaneously maintaining pressure through a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz targeting Iranian oil exports.[1] Financial markets have already reacted positively to

signals.headline: Market relevance:** The outcome of these talks before April 21 could significantly impact geopolitical risk premiums, oi
signals.raw: Market relevance:** The outcome of these talks before April 21 could significantly impact geopolitical risk premiums, oil prices, and defense sector valuations. No final agreement has been confirmed or announced—only that negotiations are ongoing with mixed signals about progress.
